subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Which of these was not a decision reached concerning the ban of the compulsory sale of handouts, course-based textbooks and practical manuals by the Senate?

options.A: Any lecturer found contravening the ban shall be expelled by the Governing Council of the University
options.B: 3-5 member task force shall be constituted to monitor the implementation of the decision
options.C: the university shall purchase twenty or more copies of textbooks by lecturers to be deposited in the main or departmental library
options.D: students caught collaborating with lecturers to contravene the ban will be subjected to disciplinary measures prescribed by the Senate

explanation: The correct answer is A. The Senate decisions on the ban typically include monitoring mechanisms, library deposits of textbooks, and disciplinary actions for students and lecturers, but expulsion by the Governing Council is not listed as a direct outcome in the policy. This promotes ethical academic practices and access to resources.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Which of these is a wrong category of courses?
explanation: The correct answer is D. RSU categorizes courses as Registered, Elective, and Compulsory. All listed are valid categories, ensuring students meet core requirements while having flexibility in electives for a balanced curriculum.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A student may withdraw from the Rivers State University with the permission of the Faculty Board and Senate, but may apply for registration within one year of the withdrawal
explanation: The correct answer is A (true). University regulations allow temporary withdrawal with approvals, providing a one-year window for re-registration to support students facing challenges while maintaining academic continuity.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: From the current web ranking of universities (webometrics) in Nigeria, the Rivers State University emerged position.
explanation: The correct answer is B. RSU's position in webometrics rankings reflects its growth as a technological university in Nigeria, highlighting improvements in research output and online presence.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: All results, excluding those of final year sub-degree and undergraduates programmes, approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the approval.

options.B: senate committee of assessment and graduation requirements

explanation: The correct answer is B. The Senate Committee of Assessment and Graduation Requirements oversees result approvals to ensure fairness and compliance with university standards for progression and graduation.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The head of Department, Dean of Faculty, Faculty Examination Officer, and Secretary to the Faculty Board retain copies of the results of all students to avoid unofficial releases of results
explanation: The correct answer is A (true). Retaining copies by key officers prevents unauthorized releases and maintains integrity in the examination and result management process.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The departmental Examination Officer collects answer booklets from the
explanation: The correct answer is B. The Faculty Examination Officer coordinates collection to centralize script handling before departmental processing, ensuring secure and organized exam administration.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A student in a four-year degree programme in the Rivers State university who is unable to complete his programme in 12 semesters maybe allowed an additional semesters, but shall be eligible to a classified degree.
explanation: The correct answer is B. Regulations allow limited extensions (e.g., 2 extra semesters) while preserving eligibility for classified degrees, balancing support for students with academic standards.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The rivers state university is the only university in Nigeria that offers first degree programmes in

options.D: forestry and environment and land surveying and chemistry

explanation: The correct answer is A. RSU stands out for unique programs like Marine Engineering, reflecting its focus as a technological university in the Niger Delta region.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The students' result considered and approved by the Faculty Board of Examiners are presented to the Dean of the Faculty
explanation: The correct answer is A (True). Faculty Board approvals are channeled through the Dean for further processing, ensuring hierarchical oversight in result management.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: All examination scripts are to be marked and submitted to the Head of Department normally within of a given examination.
explanation: The correct answer is D. A one-week window allows thorough marking while ensuring timely result processing for students.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: All results approved by the Faculty Board are submitted to the Senate Committee of Assessment and Graduation Requirements within of the last date of the reexamination for approval.
explanation: The correct answer is D. The flexible 2-4 week timeline accommodates thorough review before Senate-level approval.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A student who does not register all his courses within the period stipulated will be made to

options.A: forfeit 5% of his total class attendance in all course
options.B: pay a specific sum for late registration
options.D: suffer any penalty prescribed by the Head of Department

explanation: The correct answer is B. Late registration penalties encourage timely compliance with academic calendars.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: What is the likely punishment meted on a student who fails to sit down while invigilators are retrieving scripts from students at the end of an examination?
explanation: The correct answer is B. Such disruptions can lead to suspension to maintain order and fairness during exam conclusion.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Online registration for bed spaces for hostel accommodation as a fresh student is done with the use of
explanation: The correct answer is B. JAMB number links to admission data for fair hostel allocation.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: An external examiner approved by the Senate, is appointed each session for a maximum of sessions consecutively.
explanation: The correct answer is C. Limiting to three sessions ensures fresh perspectives and prevents conflicts of interest.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The Head of Department compiles all final results of all students and for all courses in a department within days.
explanation: The correct answer is D. The range allows flexibility while ensuring prompt compilation for Faculty Board review.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The responsibilities of the Examination Committee set up by the department includes all of the following, except

options.A: to publicize the list of invigilators
options.B: to liase with the Senate Committee on Calendar and Time-table to ensure no conflicts in courses
options.C: to prepare examination halls and sitting arrangements of students
options.D: to approve the appointment of external examiners for the department

explanation: The correct answer is D. External examiner appointments are handled at Senate level, not departmental committees.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A student is said to be on clear standing if he maintains and passes all the courses he takes in the university.
explanation: The correct answer is C. A minimum CGPA of 2.00 ensures students meet the standard for good academic standing and progression.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: All of these statements are true, except

options.A: the sale of handouts by teaching staff is an offence
options.B: all lecturers must make available office addresses and contact time to their students
options.C: teaching staff are allowed to change time tables to suit themselves as long as students are able to meet up
options.D: teaching staff cannot delegate the supervision of examinations to non-academic staff

explanation: The correct answer is C. Timetable changes require proper approval to avoid disruptions; arbitrary changes are not permitted.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The ad hoc committee on handouts and textbooks inaugurated by the Vice Chancellor was to

options.A: to consider any matters related to the compulsory sale of handouts and textbooks that may be referred to it by the Vice Chancellor
options.B: to examine the decision made by the Senate banning the compulsory sale of handouts and textbooks
options.C: to forward the names of lecturers who contravene the ban of the compulsory sale of handouts and textbooks

explanation: The correct answer is D. The committee's broad mandate supports enforcement of the ban on compulsory sales.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Lecturers who have to mark examination scripts for courses with large number of students can

options.A: delegate non-academic staff to mark scripts
options.B: supervise students in a higher level in marking the scripts
options.C: engage in conference marking of the scripts

explanation: The correct answer is C. Conference marking is permitted for efficiency, but delegation to non-academics is restricted.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Failure to clear and register online within the period specified for students will result in the following, except

options.A: affected persons will be regarded as non-students
options.B: affected persons may be allowed to defer their admission
options.C: affected persons will not attend lectures and take school examinations

explanation: The correct answer is D. Strict registration deadlines ensure all options A-C apply to maintain academic order.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A transfer from one department to another will involve all of the following except the
explanation: The correct answer is C. Transfers are handled internally by committees and Senate; Pro-Chancellor involvement is not required.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Which of these statements is not true about internal examiners in the Rivers State University?

options.A: They are of the rank of Senior Lecturers and above
options.B: they constitute the departmental or faculty board of examiners
options.C: they sign examination cards for records of lectures attended by registered students if satisfactory
options.D: they submit grades, answer scripts, marking schemes and copies of their question papers to the Chief Examiner for storage

explanation: The correct answer is A. Internal examiners may include qualified lecturers below Senior rank depending on departmental policies.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: For a five-year programme in the Rivers State University, a CCE student for Direct Entry into year three is expected to earn a minimum of credit units.
explanation: The correct answer is D. Direct Entry students must meet adjusted credit requirements to align with program duration.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Which is the largest state in Nigeria?
explanation: The correct answer is D. Borno State is the largest by land area in Nigeria.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: How many states were created by General Yakubu Gowon in Nigeria, May 1967?
explanation: The correct answer is A. Gowon created 12 states in 1967 as part of restructuring the federation.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who was the first Executive President of Nigeria?
explanation: The correct answer is B. Shehu Shagari became Nigeria's first Executive President in 1979 under the Second Republic.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: When was facebook founded?
explanation: The correct answer is A. Facebook was founded by Mark Zuckerberg and others in February 2004.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: How many regional divisions does Africa have?
explanation: The correct answer is B. Africa has five regions: Northern, Southern, Eastern, Western, and Central as per UN classification.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who is the current managing director of NNPC?
explanation: The correct answer is D. As of recent records, the Group CEO/MD has been updated; verify current holder via official sources.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who was the longest serving senate President of Nigeria?
explanation: The correct answer is B. David Mark served the longest as Senate President from 2007 to 2015.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom?
explanation: The correct answer is D. The UK PM changes; current holder as of 2026 should be verified.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
explanation: The correct answer is A. Antonio Guterres has served as UN Secretary-General since 2017.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The Senate of the Rivers State University is otherwise referred to as the
explanation: The correct answer is A. The Senate functions as the Committee of Deans in academic governance.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Following the request of a student or the Head of Department for the reassessment of a student's quality of work, the faculty is expected to meet the request within a period of ____.
explanation: The correct answer is B. Timely reassessment (typically 2 weeks) ensures prompt resolution of academic grievances.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: Who designed the Nigerian National Flag?
explanation: The correct answer is A. Michael Taiwo Akinkunmi won the 1959 design competition for Nigeria's flag.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: The capital of Rivers State is:
explanation: The correct answer is B. Port Harcourt serves as the capital and economic hub of Rivers State.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: RSU is the first Technological University in
explanation: The correct answer is A. RSU holds the distinction as Nigeria's first technological university.

subject: About RSU & Rules
text: A student must obtain a minimum CGPA of _____ for graduation in most programmes.
explanation: The correct answer is C. CGPA 2.00 is the standard threshold for degree classification eligibility.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'By Him who dominates my soul I swear that you are the Prophet of this nation... you will be abused and persecuted. If I should ever live to see that day, I shall surely help the cause of Allah.' Who made this statement?
explanation: Waraqah b. Nawfal was Khadijah's Christian cousin who, upon hearing of the Prophet's first revelation, recognized the signs of prophethood from earlier scriptures and predicted the persecution he would face. He pledged to support the Prophet's cause if he lived to see it.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The ninth year of Hijrah is known as the year of
explanation: The ninth year of Hijrah (630-631 CE) is called the 'Year of Deputations' (Aam al-Wufud) because many Arab tribal delegations came to Madinah to pledge allegiance to the Prophet and embrace Islam following the conquest of Makkah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: After the Prophet (S.A.W) migrated to al-Madinah, the first two major wars he fought against the Makkans were
explanation: The battles of Badr (624 CE) and Uhud (625 CE) were the first two major military confrontations between the Muslims of Madinah and the Makkan Quraysh after the Hijrah, with Badr being a decisive Muslim victory and Uhud a costly setback.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Quraysh boycotted Banu Hashim because they

options.C: planned to migrate to Yathrib with the Prophet
options.D: protected the Prophet (S.A.W) and refused to surrender him

explanation: The Quraysh imposed a social and economic boycott on Banu Hashim (the Prophet's clan) for about three years because the clan, though not all Muslim, refused to hand over Muhammad (S.A.W) to be killed or silenced, honouring their tribal duty of protection.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The success of the business venture which Muhammad (S.A.W) carried out for Khadijah was due mainly to

options.B: the fact that he was a prophet
options.D: the skill and hard work of his assistants

explanation: Before prophethood, Muhammad (S.A.W) was already renowned in Makkah as al-Amin (the Trustworthy) and as-Sadiq (the Truthful) because of his honesty and diligence in trade, which is what impressed Khadijah and made his trading mission for her so profitable.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The social reforms witnessed by Arabia during the lifetime of the Prophet (S.A.W) included the

options.C: modification of the practice of infanticide
options.D: universal recognition of the Arabs as the superior race

explanation: Islam did not abolish slavery outright but introduced humane reforms encouraging kindness, fair treatment, and manumission of slaves, thereby improving the relationship between masters and slaves as part of its broader social reform agenda.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The treaty of al-Hudaybiyyah was expected to last
explanation: The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, signed in 6 AH between the Prophet and the Quraysh, stipulated a ten-year truce between the two parties, though it was broken by the Quraysh after about two years, leading to the conquest of Makkah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Khulafa al-Rashidun reigned between
explanation: The period of the four Rightly Guided Caliphs (Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali) spans from 632 CE, the year the Prophet died, to 661 CE, when Ali was assassinated and Mu'awiyah established the Umayyad dynasty.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The conversion of Umar b. al-Khattab was a turning point in the history of Islam because

options.A: it encouraged the actualization of the Prophet's mission
options.B: the entire Quraysh were immediately converted to Islam
options.C: he publicly announced his migration to al-Madinah and was not challenged
options.D: the boycott of Banu Hashim was terminated

explanation: Umar's conversion gave the small Muslim community new strength and courage, allowing them to worship openly at the Ka'bah for the first time, which greatly encouraged the open propagation and actualization of the Prophet's mission.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The battle of Siffin led to the emergence of the
explanation: Following the arbitration dispute after the Battle of Siffin between Ali and Mu'awiyah, a group of Ali's own supporters rejected the arbitration and seceded, becoming known as the Khawarij ('those who went out'), the earliest sectarian group in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: During the reign of Caliph Ali, the seat of administration moved from al-Madinah to
explanation: Caliph Ali b. Abi Talib relocated the capital of the Islamic state from Madinah to Kufah in Iraq, mainly for strategic reasons related to the ongoing conflicts with Mu'awiyah and the Khawarij.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The first Qur'anic verse revealed instructed the Prophet (S.A.W) to
explanation: The very first revelation, Surah al-Alaq (96:1-5), begins with the command 'Iqra' (Read/Recite), emphasizing the centrality of knowledge and literacy at the very foundation of the Islamic message.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Surah that tells us the story of how the Ka'abah was delivered from destruction is
explanation: Surah al-Fil (The Elephant) narrates how Allah protected the Ka'bah from the army of Abrahah, who came with elephants intending to destroy it, by sending flocks of birds that pelted them with stones of baked clay.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Suratun-Nas, protection was sought from the evils of
explanation: Surah an-Nas, the final chapter of the Qur'an, is a prayer for Allah's protection specifically from the whisperings of Satan among both the jinn and mankind, the two categories of beings capable of misguiding others.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Reference to a special night is made in Surat
explanation: Surah al-Qadr describes the 'Night of Decree' (Laylatul Qadr), the night the Qur'an began to be revealed, describing it as being better than a thousand months in spiritual value.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: A Makkan Surah with the main objective of reassuring the Prophet (S.A.W) at a time of distress is
explanation: Surah ad-Duha was revealed to console the Prophet during a pause in revelation when he felt abandoned, reassuring him that Allah had not forsaken him and that his future would be better than his present.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Suratul-Kawthar concerns the Prophet's
explanation: Surah al-Kawthar was revealed to console the Prophet after his enemies mocked him for having no surviving sons, assuring him that Allah had granted him abundant good (al-Kawthar) and that his detractors would be cut off, not him.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The verse (Q. 95:8), 'Alaysa Allahu bi ahkamil hakimin' means 'Is Allah not'
explanation: This closing verse of Surah at-Tin affirms Allah's supreme justice, translating as 'Is not Allah the best/most just of judges?' It reassures believers that ultimate justice belongs to Allah alone.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The official compilation of Hadith was initiated by
explanation: The Umayyad Caliph Umar b. Abd al-Aziz, concerned about the loss of authentic Hadith through the deaths of scholars, was the first to officially order the systematic collection and written compilation of Hadith across the Muslim lands.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The difference between isnad and matn is that

options.A: matn is the chain of reporters, while isnad is their quality
options.B: isnad is the chain of reporters, while matn is their text
options.C: isnad is more important than matn
options.D: isnad refers to the reliability of reporters, while matn refers to their weaknesses

explanation: In Hadith terminology, the isnad is the chain of narrators through whom a report has been transmitted back to the Prophet, while the matn is the actual textual content or wording of the Hadith itself.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'Innama yuridu ash-shaytanu an yuqi'a baynakumul adawata wal-baghda fil khamr wal-maysir' (Q. 5:91) enumerates the evils of
explanation: This verse from Surat al-Ma'idah explains that Satan seeks to create enmity and hatred among believers through intoxicants (khamr) and gambling (maysir), both of which are strictly prohibited in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: One of the strong proofs of the authenticity of the Qur'an is that

options.A: the like of it has not been produced by an individual or a group of people
options.B: the Arabic language in which it was revealed is still spoken
options.C: it was compiled soon after its revelation
options.D: it has been translated into many languages

explanation: The Qur'an itself issues a challenge (tahaddi) that no one, individually or collectively, has ever been able to produce a chapter like it, which is considered by Muslims as the primary miraculous proof of its divine origin.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Islam, intoxicants are
explanation: The final Qur'anic ruling on intoxicants (Q. 5:90-91) declares them an abomination of Satan's handiwork to be avoided entirely, and the Prophet clarified that whatever intoxicates in large amounts is forbidden even in small amounts.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Qur'anic verse 'wala tamshi fil-ardi marahan...inna Allaha la yuhibbu kulla mukhtalin fakhur' (31:18) teaches against
explanation: In this verse of Surah Luqman, Allah warns against walking on earth with arrogance and boastfulness, declaring that He does not love anyone who is conceited and boastful.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The verse 'wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad' (Q. 112:4) says that Allah
explanation: This final verse of Surah al-Ikhlas states that there is nothing comparable or equal to Allah, emphasizing His absolute uniqueness (tawhid) and rejecting any notion of equals or partners.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: From which chapter of the Qur'an is the verse which declares 'Certainly, we created man in the best make'?
explanation: This declaration is found in Surah at-Tin (chapter 95), verse 4, which affirms that Allah created humankind in the finest and most excellent form before addressing their potential fall unless they believe and do righteous deeds.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: To maintain the quality of Taqwa one has to be
explanation: Taqwa (God-consciousness) requires continuous self-discipline in obeying Allah's commands and avoiding His prohibitions; it is a state achieved through consistent, disciplined spiritual effort rather than wealth or social status.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Sahih al-Bukhari is regarded as the most authentic collection of Hadith because its collector was

options.A: older than any other collector of Hadith
options.B: alive during the time of the Prophet (S.A.W)
options.C: the most critical of the collectors
options.D: a founder of a school of law

explanation: Imam Bukhari applied the strictest criteria in verifying the reliability of narrators and the continuity of chains of transmission, making his collection, Sahih al-Bukhari, the most rigorously authenticated Hadith compilation in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Musnad is a collection of ahadith arranged

options.A: according to the names of their authorities
options.B: into chapters dealing with specific topics

explanation: Unlike topical (musannaf) collections, a Musnad arranges Hadith according to the individual Companion who narrated them, grouping all traditions transmitted through a particular narrator together, as in Musnad Ahmad b. Hanbal.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Part of the 23rd Hadith of al-Nawawi's collection refers to purification as a
explanation: Hadith 23 of an-Nawawi's collection begins with the Prophet's saying 'Purity (taharah) is half of faith,' highlighting the central importance of physical and spiritual cleanliness in the practice of Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following statements completes the Hadith which says 'He is not a faithful Muslim who eats to his fill while...'?

options.A: he is on a journey in the month of Ramadan
options.B: his neighbour is hungry beside him
options.D: his colleagues are waiting for him

explanation: This Hadith teaches social responsibility and compassion, condemning a Muslim who is satisfied and well-fed while a neighbour nearby goes hungry, emphasizing communal care in Islamic ethics.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: A Hadith with uninterrupted chain of authorities traced back to the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) himself is described as
explanation: A Hadith Mutawatir is one reported by such a large number of narrators at every stage of transmission, all the way back to the Prophet, that it is impossible for them to have conspired to fabricate it, making it the highest degree of authenticity.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following terms is most closely related to divorce?
explanation: Iddah is the mandatory waiting period a woman must observe after divorce (or the death of her husband) before she can remarry, allowing time to confirm she is not pregnant and to allow for possible reconciliation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Kaffarah (major expiation) is compulsory upon a husband who revokes
explanation: Zihar is the pre-Islamic practice of a husband likening his wife's back to that of his mother to make her forbidden to him; if he later wishes to resume marital relations, Islamic law requires him to pay a specific kaffarah (expiation).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Part of the wisdom in instituting the iddah in marriage is to

options.B: enable the wife to settle down

explanation: Among the key wisdoms of the iddah period is that it provides a waiting time during which the couple, especially in cases of revocable divorce (talaq raj'i), may reconsider and reconcile before the divorce becomes final.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Zakah rate to be paid on money is
explanation: The standard rate of Zakah payable on money and monetary assets that have reached the nisab (minimum threshold) and been held for a full lunar year is 2.5%, as established by the Prophet's teaching and consensus of scholars.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: To ensure the authenticity of the compiled text of the Holy Qur'an, extracts written on various materials were cross-checked with texts

options.B: recalled from memory by early memorizers
options.C: delivered by angel Jibril to the Prophet (S.A.W)

explanation: During the compilation under Abu Bakr, Zayd b. Thabit cross-checked all written fragments of the Qur'an against the memorization of the Huffaz (those who had memorized the Qur'an from the Prophet), ensuring accuracy through dual verification.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following terms portrays the Qur'an as a healing for man's spiritual and moral waywardness?
explanation: Among the names given to the Qur'an is ash-Shifa' (the Healer), reflecting its role in curing spiritual diseases such as doubt, hypocrisy, and moral corruption, as mentioned in Q. 10:57.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Suratul-Ma'un (Q.107), we learn that a good believer should not
explanation: Surah al-Ma'un opens by describing the one who denies the religion as someone who repulses the orphan and does not encourage feeding the poor, teaching that true faith must be reflected in compassionate social conduct.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The expressions ra'a, 'allama and al-'alam in Suratul-'Alaq emphasize the importance of
explanation: Surah al-'Alaq, the first revealed chapter, repeatedly stresses the acquisition of knowledge through terms like 'read' (iqra), 'taught' (allama), and 'the pen' (al-qalam), establishing learning as central to Islam from its very inception.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'It is most hateful in the sight of Allah that you say that which you do not' (Q. 61:3). The main teaching of the verse above is honesty in
explanation: This verse condemns saying what one does not do, teaching that a believer's words and actions must be consistent, uniting honesty of speech with honesty of practice as an essential feature of true faith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Imam Malik was born in
explanation: Imam Malik b. Anas, founder of the Maliki school of law, was born and spent his entire life in Madinah, the city whose living tradition and practice (amal ahl al-Madinah) formed a major source of his jurisprudence.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In the Islamic belief system, the opposite of Tawhid is
explanation: Tawhid is the belief in the absolute oneness of Allah, while Shirk is its direct opposite—the sin of associating partners with Allah in His divinity, worship, or attributes, considered the gravest sin in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: According to Islamic teachings, angels are
explanation: Angels in Islam are created beings of light who have no free will to disobey and are entirely devoted to worship and carrying out Allah's commands, making them completely obedient and loyal servants.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The prophet whose son was drowned in a flood along with the unbelievers was
explanation: Prophet Nuh (Noah) preached to his people for centuries, and when the great flood came as punishment for their disbelief, even his own son, who refused to board the ark and heed his father's call, was among those drowned.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Fortune telling is un-Islamic

options.A: only when it is done by non-Muslims
options.B: when Islamic scriptures are not used for it
options.C: only in matters relating to religious practices
options.D: at all times and in all matters

explanation: Islam categorically forbids fortune-telling and seeking knowledge of the unseen from soothsayers under all circumstances, as only Allah possesses complete knowledge of the unseen; the Prophet warned that consulting fortune tellers invalidates one's prayers for forty days.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The second Hadith in an-Nawawi's collection teaches that it is righteousness to worship Allah as if

options.A: one would die the following day
options.B: He would punish one on the spot

explanation: This is the famous Hadith of Jibril describing 'Ihsan' (excellence in faith), where the Prophet defines it as worshipping Allah as though you see Him, and if you cannot achieve that level, knowing that He surely sees you.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: A woman in her menstrual period

options.A: is not exempted from all forms of ibadah
options.B: is exempted from all forms of ibadah
options.D: can only perform the obligatory prayers

explanation: While a menstruating woman is exempted from Salat and Sawm (fasting), she is not exempted from all forms of worship; she may still engage in dhikr (remembrance of Allah), du'a (supplication), and charitable acts.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The doctrine of trinity is regarded in Islam as a form of
explanation: The Christian doctrine of the Trinity, which associates other beings with the Godhead, is regarded in Islam as shirk—the sin of ascribing partners to Allah—which directly contradicts the fundamental Islamic principle of Tawhid.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The practice of comparing the back of one's wife to that of one's mother as a method of divorce was known as
explanation: Zihar was a pre-Islamic Arab custom in which a husband would declare his wife's back to be like that of his mother, thereby making sexual relations with her forbidden; Islam regulated this practice by requiring expiation before resumption of marital relations.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Islamic law of inheritance, the brother of a deceased person who had a son is to

options.A: take a larger share of the inheritance
options.B: take half of the share of the son
options.C: get equal share with the son

explanation: In Islamic inheritance law, closer relatives (asabah) block more distant ones from inheriting; since a son is a closer heir than a brother, the presence of a son excludes the deceased's brother entirely from the estate.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The shariah contrasts with the common law because the former is
explanation: Unlike common law, which evolves through changing precedents and legislation, the Shariah is based on divinely revealed sources (Qur'an and Sunnah) that are considered fixed and immutable in their core principles, even as scholars apply them to new situations.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In the Islamic economic system, baytul mal is equivalent to the
explanation: Baytul Mal refers to the public treasury of the Islamic state, established to collect and manage revenues such as zakah, jizyah, and kharaj, and to disburse them for the welfare of the Muslim community.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The pre-Islamic practice of shigar which was abolished by Islam meant
explanation: Nikah al-shighar was a pre-Islamic marriage custom where two men would exchange their daughters or sisters in marriage without a proper dowry, treating women as bargaining objects; the Prophet explicitly prohibited this practice.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Imam Ash-Shafi'i studied under two of his predecessors. These are
explanation: Imam ash-Shafi'i, founder of the Shafi'i school, studied jurisprudence directly under Imam Malik in Madinah and was also influenced by the teachings of the Hanafi school established by Imam Abu Hanifah, synthesizing elements of both traditions.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Ignorance of religious rites and duties is not excusable in Islam because

options.A: it is obligatory on all Muslims to seek knowledge
options.B: most Muslims can read and write Arabic
options.C: books of Islamic jurisprudence have been translated into many languages
options.D: the Islamic system of education is highly developed all over the world

explanation: The Prophet declared that seeking knowledge is obligatory upon every Muslim (talab al-'ilm faridatun 'ala kulli Muslim), so a Muslim's failure to learn basic religious duties cannot be excused as they are required to actively pursue this knowledge.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Al-bayyinah appears in the Holy Qur'an, chapter 98,
explanation: The word 'al-bayyinah' (the clear proof/evidence) appears twice in Surah al-Bayyinah (chapter 98), reflecting the chapter's theme of the undeniable clarity of the message brought by the Prophet.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The major subject of the first revelation to Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) is
explanation: The first revelation in Surah al-Alaq centres on education—commanding the Prophet to 'Read' and highlighting that Allah taught man by the pen and taught him what he knew not, establishing knowledge as foundational to the message of Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'Of the excellence of the Islam of a man is his leaving alone what does not concern him.' The above statement is a
explanation: This well-known saying is a Hadith of the Prophet (S.A.W), reported in an-Nawawi's collection, teaching that a sign of a person's excellence in Islam is minding their own affairs and avoiding unnecessary interference in matters that do not concern them.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Surah which declares that there is no compromise in religion is
explanation: Surah al-Kafirun explicitly declares the Prophet's rejection of any compromise with the polytheists' religion, ending with the famous statement 'lakum dinukum wa liya din' — 'To you your religion, and to me my religion.'

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Islam regards the whole body of a woman as 'awrah except her face and
explanation: According to the majority view in Islamic jurisprudence, a woman's entire body is considered 'awrah (to be covered) in front of non-mahram men, with the common exception being her face and hands.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following was the most preferred method of preserving the Holy Qur'an during the time of the rightly guided caliphs?
explanation: Memorization (hifz) by the Huffaz was the most trusted and preferred method of preserving the Qur'an during the early caliphate, complemented by written copies which were cross-verified against the memorized text.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The collections regarded as the most authentic in Hadith literature are those of
explanation: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim are collectively known as 'the two Sahihs' (as-Sahihain) and are universally regarded by Muslim scholars as the two most authentic Hadith collections after the Qur'an itself.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Muwatta' of Imam Malik can be described as a book of
explanation: The Muwatta' of Imam Malik is unique among early Hadith works because it combines authentic Hadith reports with the Imam's own legal rulings and the established practice of Madinah, making it both a Hadith and Fiqh text.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The attribute of Allah which emphasizes His holiness is
explanation: Al-Quddus, meaning 'The Most Holy' or 'The Pure One,' is the divine name that specifically emphasizes Allah's absolute purity and transcendence above any imperfection or deficiency.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The angel that interrogates the dead in the grave is
explanation: According to Islamic teaching, two angels named Munkar and Nakir visit the deceased in the grave to question them about their faith, deeds, and knowledge of the Prophet, a stage known as the 'punishment or bliss of the grave' (adhab/na'im al-qabr).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Ibrahim is described in the Holy Qur'an as
explanation: The Qur'an describes Prophet Ibrahim as a 'hanif,' meaning one who was naturally inclined to pure monotheism, predating both Judaism and Christianity, since he lived before either religion was established (Q. 3:67).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: With the institution of zakah, the distribution of wealth within the society should become
explanation: Zakah does not aim to make everyone's wealth equal but ensures a proportional redistribution, where a fixed percentage of the wealthy's surplus wealth is channelled to the poor and needy, promoting balance without eliminating economic differences entirely.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In which year of the Hijrah was fasting introduced in Islam?
explanation: Fasting in the month of Ramadan was made obligatory upon Muslims in the second year after the Hijrah (2 AH), following the revelation of Q. 2:183, coinciding roughly with the time of the Battle of Badr.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Zihar means

options.A: likening a wife's back to that of one's mother
options.B: forcing a woman to marry against her wish

explanation: Zihar is defined as a husband's statement comparing his wife's back to his mother's back (a form of forbidden pre-Islamic divorce-like pronouncement), which Islam regulated by requiring specific expiation before marital relations could resume.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Islamic law of inheritance, the share of the female child is
explanation: According to Q. 4:11, when children inherit together, a female child generally receives half the share of a male child, reflecting the differing financial obligations placed upon men and women under Islamic law.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In the law of inheritance, the term al-wasiyyah refers to
explanation: Al-wasiyyah is a Muslim's will or bequest, allowing a person to allocate up to one-third of their estate to non-heirs or charitable causes, made before their death and executed after.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In the Islamic political system, Mas'uliyyah means
explanation: Mas'uliyyah refers to the principle of accountability in the Islamic political system, whereby leaders are answerable both to the people they govern and ultimately to Allah for the exercise of their authority.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Prophet (S.A.W) died in the year
explanation: Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) passed away in 632 CE (11 AH) in Madinah, after completing his mission of prophethood which began with the first revelation in 610 CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Muslim ruler of Mali who embarked on a celebrated pilgrimage of Makkah in the 11th Century C.E was
explanation: Mansa Musa, the ruler of the Mali Empire, undertook a famous pilgrimage to Makkah, during which his lavish distribution of gold made him internationally renowned across Africa and the Middle East.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The first male adult to embrace Islam after the commencement of Islamic da'wah in Makkah was
explanation: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq is widely recognized as the first free adult male to accept Islam after the Prophet began his mission, becoming one of his closest and most trusted companions.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The man who analyzed the first encounter of the Prophet (S.A.W) with Angel Jibril was
explanation: When the Prophet was terrified after his first encounter with Angel Jibril in the Cave of Hira, Khadijah took him to her cousin, Waraqah b. Nawfal, a Christian scholar, who explained the significance of the encounter as divine revelation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Fount (of abundance) was given to the Prophet (S.A.W) and made known to him in Surah
explanation: Surah al-Kawthar informs the Prophet that Allah has granted him 'al-Kawthar,' understood as a river in Paradise as well as abundant goodness, as consolation for the losses and mockery he suffered.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The basic theme of Surah al-Zilzal is the description of

options.A: fearful events of the Day of Judgment
options.B: how people will be rewarded with paradise
options.D: punishment of the unbelievers in hell

explanation: Surah al-Zilzal (The Earthquake) vividly describes the terrifying convulsions of the earth on the Day of Judgment, when it will shake violently and every soul's deeds, however small, will be made manifest.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Sahaba were the first to embark on the interpretation of the Glorious Qur'an in order to

options.A: be known as teachers of Tafsir
options.B: bring out the treasures in the Glorious Qur'an
options.D: get used to the language of the Glorious Qur'an

explanation: The Companions began interpreting the Qur'an not for personal fame but to unlock and explain the deep meanings and treasures embedded within the revealed text for the benefit of the wider Muslim community.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The term 'Asma ar-Rijal' refers to the investigation of the
explanation: 'Ilm Asma ar-Rijal (the science of the men) is the field of Hadith studies concerned with critically examining the biographies, reliability, and character of Hadith narrators to determine the authenticity of the reports they transmitted.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The term al-Mutawatir refers to Hadith narrated by
explanation: Hadith Mutawatir refers to reports transmitted through so many independent chains and narrators at each stage that collusion in fabrication becomes practically impossible, granting it certain (qat'i) authenticity.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Prophet (S.A.W) enjoins that if anyone sees an abomination, the least thing to do is to
explanation: In this famous Hadith on enjoining good and forbidding evil, the Prophet outlines three levels of response: changing it with the hand, then with the tongue, and if neither is possible, hating it in one's heart, which is the weakest level of faith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Q. 2:219 prohibits wine and gambling for the Muslims because

options.A: they lead to extravagance and intoxication
options.B: the sins in them are greater than the profits
options.D: they lead one to ignore one's religion

explanation: This verse states that in wine and gambling there is great sin as well as some benefit for people, but the sin involved is far greater than the benefit, which was the stated reason for their eventual prohibition.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The forbidden types of food listed in Surah 2:173 include carrion, blood, swine flesh and that which is
explanation: Q. 2:173 lists four categories of forbidden food: carrion (maytah), blood, the flesh of swine, and any animal that has been slaughtered while invoking a name other than Allah's, i.e., dedicated to idols or other gods.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The admonition against arrogance in Surah 31:18 is directed to the son of
explanation: Surah Luqman records the wise counsel of Luqman to his son, including the warning against arrogant walking and boastful conduct, forming part of a broader set of ethical and spiritual advice.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: '...here are my daughters: they are purer for you'... (Q.11:78). The statement above was made by Prophet Lut to discourage
explanation: Prophet Lut made this offer to his people in an attempt to redirect them from the sin of same-sex relations, which was the widespread and unprecedented abomination practiced by his community, towards lawful marriage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Ar-Rahman as one of the attributes of Allah implies that He is
explanation: Ar-Rahman, meaning 'The Most Merciful/Compassionate,' describes Allah's all-encompassing mercy and kindness extended to all His creation, believers and non-believers alike, in this world.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The word Hafizin in Q.82:10 refers to
explanation: In Q. 82:10-11, 'Hafizin' (guardians/watchers) refers to the recording angels appointed over every person, noble scribes who diligently record all their deeds.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Surah 79:24 reveals that Pharaoh's action is incompatible with the Islamic principle of Tawhid because he
explanation: In Q. 79:24, Pharaoh declared 'Ana rabbukumul a'la' ('I am your lord, most high'), directly claiming divinity for himself, an ultimate act of self-deification that stands in complete opposition to the core Islamic doctrine of Tawhid.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Jihad against carnal self is a
explanation: According to a well-known Prophetic tradition, the struggle against one's own base desires and ego (jihad an-nafs) is described as the 'greater jihad' (al-jihad al-akbar), considered more challenging than physical combat, which is termed the lesser jihad.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The period of custody of a baby girl after her mother has been divorced lasts until she is
explanation: In many schools of Islamic law, a divorced mother retains custody (hadanah) of her daughter for a longer period than her son, generally until the girl reaches marriageable age, reflecting the greater protective needs of a female child.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'If ye do it not, take notice of war from Allah and His Apostle: But if ye turn back, ye shall have your capital sums…' (Q.2:279). The quotation above warns against
explanation: This verse is part of the Qur'anic prohibition of riba (usury/interest), warning those who persist in taking interest that they are effectively declaring war against Allah and His Messenger, while allowing them to reclaim only their original capital.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Who among the prophets of Allah was sent to his people to warn against deceit in terms of measurement and weight?
explanation: Prophet Shuaib was sent to the people of Madyan, who were notorious for cheating in weights and measures during trade; he repeatedly warned them to give full measure and weight with justice.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Who among the four rightly guided caliphs introduced the system of Bayt al-mal?
explanation: Caliph Umar b. al-Khattab established the Bayt al-mal (public treasury) as a formal institution to collect and systematically distribute state revenues, including stipends for soldiers and citizens, marking a significant administrative innovation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The first man on earth, Adam (A.S), was exalted above other creatures because of his
explanation: According to Q. 2:31-33, Allah taught Adam the names of all things, and when Adam demonstrated this knowledge before the angels who could not, it established the superiority granted to him by virtue of this divinely-taught knowledge.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Wahy in relation to the Glorious Qur'an means
explanation: Wahy technically means divine revelation—the process by which Allah communicated His message to His prophets, most notably through Angel Jibril, culminating in the Qur'an being revealed to Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Makkan suwar lay emphasis on oneness of Allah because

options.A: it was the only way to call Makkans to Islam
options.B: it is the only article of faith
options.C: the pre-Islamic Makkans worshipped many gods
options.D: it is the basis of iman and other articles of faith

explanation: The Makkan chapters strongly emphasize Tawhid because the primary religious problem confronting the early Muslim community in Makkah was the widespread polytheism (shirk) practiced by the pagan Quraysh and other Arab tribes.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The uniqueness of the Glorious Qur'an, according to Q.39:28, lies in the fact that it is
explanation: Q. 39:28 describes the Qur'an as 'an Arabic Qur'an without any crookedness,' highlighting its clarity and eloquence in the Arabic language as one of its distinguishing features.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: One of the sahabah considered most knowledgeable in Tafsir was
explanation: Abdullah b. Abbas, cousin of the Prophet, was specially prayed for by the Prophet to be granted deep understanding of religion, and he became renowned among the Companions as the foremost authority in Qur'anic interpretation, earning the title 'Turjuman al-Qur'an'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The contents of Surah al-Fatihah can be summed up to mean that

options.B: man must always fear Allah and obey Him
options.C: man must worship Allah and seek His help
options.D: it is the first Surah that was revealed

explanation: Surah al-Fatihah, recited in every unit of prayer, centres on the declaration 'Iyyaka na'budu wa iyyaka nasta'in' ('You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help'), summarizing the core relationship of worship and dependence between man and Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The creation of man in orderly proportion is described in Surah
explanation: Surah at-Tin (95:4) states that Allah created man 'in the best of stature (proportion),' emphasizing the excellence and order with which humankind was fashioned before addressing the consequences of disbelief.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Az-zabaniyah in the verse 'sanad'u'iz-zabaniyah' refers to the
explanation: Az-Zabaniyah, mentioned in Surah al-'Alaq, refers to the fierce angels of punishment appointed to seize and torment wrongdoers, and the verse warns that they will be summoned against the arrogant oppressor.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following Hadith books emphasizes the practice of the people of Madinah?
explanation: The Muwatta' of Imam Malik uniquely gives special weight to the living practice ('amal) of the people of Madinah as a source of law, since Madinah was the city of the Prophet where the Sunnah was most authentically preserved and practiced.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Musnad as a work on Hadith is credited to
explanation: Imam Ahmad b. Hanbal, founder of the Hanbali school, compiled the famous 'Musnad Ahmad,' a vast collection of Hadith arranged according to the Companion who narrated each report.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: According to Hadith 6 of an-Nawawi, it is better to avoid ambiguous matters in order to
explanation: Hadith 6 of an-Nawawi's collection teaches that one who guards against doubtful matters protects both his religion and his honour, while one who falls into doubtful matters risks falling into what is clearly forbidden.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The number of lashes prescribed for the consumption of alcohol is
explanation: Based on precedent set during the caliphate of Umar b. al-Khattab following consultation with the Companions, the punishment for consuming alcohol (shurb al-khamr) was fixed at eighty lashes.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Al-'awrah for a man is from the
explanation: According to the majority of Islamic jurists, a man's 'awrah (parts that must be covered) extends from the navel to the knee, which must be covered both during prayer and in the presence of others.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Q. 24:30 enjoins Muslims to
explanation: Q. 24:30 commands believing men (and a parallel verse commands believing women) to lower their gaze and guard their modesty as a means of protecting society from illicit desire and immorality.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: According to the Prophet (S.A.W), he who betrays trust is regarded as having
explanation: The Prophet stated that one who is not trustworthy has no faith, and one who does not keep his promises has no religion, indicating that trustworthiness and honouring commitments are essential markers of both faith and religion.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The messenger of Allah sent to the people of Thamud was
explanation: Prophet Salih was sent to the people of Thamud, who were given the miraculous she-camel as a sign; when they defiantly killed it, they were destroyed by a mighty blast as punishment for their disbelief and disobedience.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Yawmuddin refers to the day of
explanation: Yawmuddin ('the Day of Recompense/Judgment') refers to the Day of Judgment when all people will be resurrected and held accountable for their deeds, as mentioned repeatedly throughout the Qur'an, including in Surah al-Fatihah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Taking the ritual bath for Friday prayers is
explanation: Ghusl (the ritual bath) before attending Friday congregational prayers is regarded by the majority of scholars as an emphasized Sunnah (recommended practice), based on the Prophet's consistent encouragement, though not obligatory for validity of the prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The number of takbir observed during salat al-Janazah is
explanation: The funeral prayer (Salat al-Janazah) consists of four takbirs, with specific supplications recited after each, and unlike ordinary prayer, it has no ruku' or sujud.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The next place for a Muslim to spend the night after 'Arafat is
explanation: After the standing at 'Arafat (wuquf), pilgrims proceed to Muzdalifah where they spend the night, perform combined Maghrib and Isha prayers, and collect pebbles for the stoning ritual at Mina the next day.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The ill-treatment of a wife in Islam is called
explanation: Shiqaq refers to marital discord or ill-treatment/conflict between spouses in Islamic family law, a situation which Islamic jurisprudence provides mechanisms (such as arbitration) to resolve before resorting to divorce.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The forms of revelations are explained in
explanation: Q. 42:51 explains the three ways Allah communicates with humans: through direct inspiration (wahy), from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger (angel) to reveal by His permission, which is how the Qur'an was revealed to the Prophet.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The type of Tafsir which uses the Qur'an to explain the Qur'an began during the time of the
explanation: Tafsir al-Qur'an bil-Qur'an (interpreting the Qur'an with the Qur'an itself) began with the Prophet, who often explained one verse by reference to another, setting the earliest precedent for this method of exegesis.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'Allah swears by the Fig and the Olive' in Surah
explanation: Surah at-Tin ('The Fig') opens with Allah swearing by the fig and the olive, symbols associated with lands of earlier prophets, before addressing the excellent creation of man and the consequences of disbelief.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Suratul-Fatihah is also referred to as
explanation: Surah al-Fatihah is famously known as 'Ummul-Kitab' (the Mother of the Book) because it summarizes the core themes of the entire Qur'an, including worship, guidance, and the relationship between Allah and humanity.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Ayatul-Kursiyy not only teaches the oneness of Allah but also emphasizes His
explanation: Ayat al-Kursi (Q. 2:255) begins by describing Allah as 'Al-Hayyul-Qayyum' — the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence — emphasizing His eternal, unceasing existence alongside His absolute oneness and dominion.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Taqrir, as a kind of Sunnah, means the

options.A: sayings of the Prophet (S.A.W) on religious matters
options.B: action or practice of the Prophet (S.A.W)
options.C: direct approach of the Prophet (S.A.W) to issues
options.D: Prophet's (S.A.W) silent approval of the actions of others

explanation: Sunnah Taqririyyah refers to instances where the Prophet witnessed a Companion's action or statement and remained silent without objecting, which is understood as his tacit approval and thus a valid source of Islamic law.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The author of the Muwatta was the Imam of
explanation: Imam Malik, author of the Muwatta', was known as the Imam of Darul-Hijrah, a title given to Madinah as the city to which the Prophet migrated and made his adopted homeland.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Hadith 34 of an-Nawawi teaches that a good Muslim should
explanation: Hadith 34 teaches that whoever among Muslims sees a wrong should change it with his hand; if unable, with his tongue (speaking against it); and if still unable, at least with his heart, which is the weakest degree of faith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The final and total prohibition of intoxicants is contained in Surah
explanation: The complete and final prohibition of alcohol came through Q. 5:90-91 in Surah al-Ma'idah, describing intoxicants and gambling as an abomination from Satan's handiwork to be totally avoided, concluding the gradual, staged process of prohibition.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The minimum amount that may be stolen to warrant cutting off the hand is
explanation: According to the majority view among Islamic jurists based on Hadith, the minimum value of stolen property (nisab) that warrants the hadd punishment of hand amputation is a quarter of a gold Dinar or its equivalent value.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Forbidding Munkar in Islam means discouraging others from
explanation: 'Al-Munkar' refers to that which is reprehensible or disapproved of by Islamic law, so 'forbidding munkar' (nahy 'anil munkar) is the duty of discouraging others from committing abominable and sinful acts.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The text of talbiyah in hajj emphasizes the pilgrim's

options.A: readiness to observe the rites of hajj to the end
options.B: realization of the uniqueness of the rites of hajj

explanation: The Talbiyah recited by pilgrims ('Labbaik Allahumma labbaik...') repeatedly affirms that all praise, blessing, and dominion belong to Allah alone, expressing the pilgrim's recognition of Allah's absolute sovereignty and authority.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Qur'an 2:193 enjoins believers to fight unbelievers in the cause of Allah if it takes that to
explanation: Q. 2:193 instructs believers to fight against oppression and persecution (fitnah) until religious freedom is secured, framing warfare in Islam within a defensive context against transgression, not as unrestricted aggression.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'The parties should either hold together on equitable terms or...' (Q.2:229). This verse of the Glorious Qur'an stresses the need for a Muslim to be kind to his wife at the time of
explanation: Q. 2:229 addresses the process of revocable divorce, instructing that a husband must either retain his wife honourably or release her with kindness, prohibiting harsh or unjust treatment during the process of separation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The major difference among the Sunni schools of law borders on
explanation: The four Sunni schools of law (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, and Hanbali) share the same foundational sources (Qur'an and Sunnah) but differ mainly in their methodologies and interpretations (ijtihad) when deriving specific legal rulings.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The most important right which both Muslims and non-Muslims have under the Islamic political system is that of
explanation: Islamic political theory guarantees justice ('adl) to all subjects of the Islamic state regardless of their religion, as fairness in governance and legal treatment is considered a fundamental right owed to everyone under Muslim rule.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Caliph Abu Bakr reigned for
explanation: Abu Bakr as-Siddiq served as the first caliph of Islam from 632 to 634 CE, a reign of approximately two years, during which he dealt with the Wars of Apostasy (Riddah) and initiated the compilation of the Qur'an.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The man who established the Muslim city of Qairawan was
explanation: Uqbah b. Nafi', a general of the Umayyad Caliphate, founded the city of Qairawan in present-day Tunisia in 670 CE, which became an important centre for the spread of Islam and Arab culture in North Africa.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The complete Glorious Qur'an was taken from Lawh al-Mahfuz to the lowest of the heavens known as
explanation: According to Islamic tradition, the entire Qur'an was first sent down all at once from the Preserved Tablet (Lawh al-Mahfuz) to Bait al-Izzah in the lowest heaven, from where it was subsequently revealed to the Prophet piecemeal over 23 years.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The most important ways of preserving the Glorious Qur'an by the Sahabah include

options.A: establishing schools in major cities and teaching the Glorious Qur'an
options.B: teaching the application of sunnah practically
options.C: explaining the conduct of the prophet according to the Glorious Qur'an
options.D: guiding the new converts in Islamic ways of life

explanation: Beyond memorization and writing, the Companions preserved the Qur'an by establishing centres of learning across the expanding Islamic territories, systematically teaching the Qur'an to new generations and converts.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: What are the characteristics common to most Makkan Suwar?
explanation: Makkan chapters are generally characterized by short, rhythmic verses with powerful, poetic language, focusing on themes such as Tawhid, the Hereafter, and moral admonition, in contrast to the longer, legislative Madinan chapters.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Glorious Qur'an is a guidance for
explanation: Q. 2:2 describes the Qur'an as 'a guidance for the pious/God-conscious' (hudan lil-muttaqin), indicating that while it addresses all humanity, its guidance is most effectively received by those with sincere God-consciousness.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Every human being, according to Surah al-Asr, is in a state of loss, except the
explanation: Surah al-Asr declares that all mankind is in loss except those who believe, do righteous deeds, and enjoin each other to truth and patience, encapsulating the criteria for a successful life in a concise formula.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The verse that can be recited for protection against evil machinations is
explanation: Q. 2:255, Ayat al-Kursi, is widely recited by Muslims for protection, as the Prophet taught that reciting it, particularly before sleep, safeguards a person from harm, including the influence of Satan.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The official compilation of Hadith was facilitated by
explanation: Umar b. Abdul-Aziz, an Umayyad caliph noted for his piety and reforms, issued the official directive ordering scholars across the Islamic world to collect and compile the Hadith in writing, fearing their loss as the generation of narrators passed away.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The science of Asma al-rijal is introduced in order to

options.A: authenticate the chains of Hadith narrators
options.B: confirm the names of Hadith books
options.D: explain the contents of the sunnah

explanation: The science of Asma al-Rijal was developed specifically to scrutinize the biography, integrity, and memory of each narrator in a Hadith's chain of transmission, forming the basis for judging whether a Hadith is authentic or weak.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The real name of Imam al-Bukhari is
explanation: Imam al-Bukhari's full name is Muhammad b. Isma'il b. Ibrahim al-Bukhari, the celebrated compiler of Sahih al-Bukhari, considered the most authentic book of Hadith after the Qur'an.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: A Hadith in which the chain of narration and text are sound is known as
explanation: A Sahih Hadith is one that meets the highest standard of authenticity: a continuous, unbroken chain of trustworthy and precise narrators, with a text free from any hidden defect or contradiction with more reliable reports.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Hadith is important to Muslims because it is

options.B: the most reliable sayings of the prophet (SAW)
options.C: the only linkage between the prophet (SAW) and the Muslim
options.D: the means through which the prophet's message is known

explanation: Hadith serves as the primary means through which the sayings, actions, and approvals of the Prophet are recorded and transmitted, allowing Muslims to understand and practically apply his teachings and example (Sunnah) in their lives.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Which of the following is the correct order of authenticity of three of the six sound collections of Hadith?
explanation: Among the six canonical Hadith collections, the traditional ranking of authenticity places Sahih al-Bukhari first, followed by Sahih Muslim, then Jami' at-Tirmidhi, reflecting the collectors' varying degrees of rigour in their acceptance criteria.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The first major written work of ahadith was
explanation: Compiled before Bukhari and Muslim's collections, the Muwatta' of Imam Malik is generally regarded as the earliest major written work systematically compiling Hadith alongside the practice of the people of Madinah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Hadith 11 of an-Nawawi admonishes Muslims to

options.A: be certain in some acts of worship
options.C: note that doubt and uncertainty are close to each other

explanation: Hadith 11 of an-Nawawi's collection, based on the Prophet's saying 'Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt,' teaches Muslims to avoid doubtful or ambiguous matters in favour of clear certainty.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: According to Hadith 41 of an-Nawawi, the faith of a believer is incomplete until he

options.A: loves for his brother what he loves for himself
options.C: likes good things for his family
options.D: follows what has been revealed unto the prophet (SAW)

explanation: Hadith 41 states, 'None of you truly believes until his desires are subservient to what I have brought,' teaching that complete faith requires submitting one's personal inclinations to the guidance revealed to the Prophet.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Q. 3:103 enjoins Muslims to
explanation: Q. 3:103 commands believers to 'hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and be not divided,' establishing unity among Muslims as a core religious duty essential to communal strength and harmony.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was described as the seal of all prophets, meaning that he

options.A: was the last of the prophets of Allah
options.B: covered all parts of the world preaching
options.C: is revered even by the Jews

explanation: The title 'Khatam an-Nabiyyin' (Seal of the Prophets), mentioned in Q. 33:40, signifies that Muhammad (S.A.W) is the final prophet sent by Allah, after whom no further prophet will come.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The type of Zakah due at the end of Ramadan fast is known as
explanation: Zakah al-Fitr is a special, obligatory charity given by every Muslim at the end of Ramadan, before the Eid prayer, intended to purify the fasting person and provide for the needy so they can also celebrate the festival.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The categories of women prohibited in marriage due to blood relations are
explanation: According to Islamic law derived from Q. 4:23, ten categories of close female blood relatives (mahram) are permanently prohibited in marriage, including mothers, daughters, sisters, aunts, and nieces.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The caliph who introduced stipend for teachers was
explanation: Caliph Umar b. al-Khattab introduced a system of stipends (salaries) drawn from the Bayt al-mal for teachers, soldiers, and other public servants, formalizing state support for education and administration.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The revelation of the Glorious Qur'an started with a
explanation: The very first revelation began with the command 'Iqra' ('Read/Recite') in Surah al-Alaq, establishing the imperative tone of the divine call from the outset.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Prophet Muhammad's (SAW) answer to Angel Jibril when he was asked to recite was
explanation: When Angel Jibril commanded the Prophet to 'Read/Recite' (Iqra), he responded 'Ma ana bi qari' ('I cannot read'), since he was unlettered, before Jibril embraced him and repeated the command, followed by the revelation of the first verses.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: How many caliphs did the Glorious Qur'an pass through before its standardization?
explanation: The Qur'an's compilation and standardization process passed through the caliphates of Abu Bakr (initial compilation), Umar (custodianship), and Uthman (final standardization into a single authoritative text), a span of three caliphs.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The authenticity of the Glorious Qur'an has been proven by empirical evidence of

options.B: stages of growth of human embryo

explanation: Modern scientific discoveries about the detailed developmental stages of the human embryo, as described in Q. 23:12-14, are frequently cited by Muslim scholars as evidence of the Qur'an's divine origin, given the precise descriptions predating microscopic embryology.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The copy of the Glorious Qur'an in use was standardized during the period of Caliph
explanation: Caliph Uthman b. Affan, concerned about growing variations in recitation across the expanding Islamic empire, appointed a committee led by Zayd b. Thabit to produce a single standardized written text of the Qur'an, which remains the basis of all copies today.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Al-Kawthar in the Glorious Qur'an is otherwise called a
explanation: Al-Kawthar is commonly understood by Islamic scholars as a special river granted to the Prophet in Paradise, described in Hadith as having banks of pearls and water whiter than milk and sweeter than honey.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The significant message contained in Surah at-Takathur is that

options.A: passion for wealth and position may divert man's attention from worship of Allah
options.B: moral obligation and spiritual sacrifice should be uppermost in the minds of a true believer
options.C: man should avoid committing sin at all times
options.D: man should pray five times daily

explanation: Surah at-Takathur warns that excessive rivalry in accumulating wealth and boasting of possessions distracts people from remembering Allah, until they are confronted with the reality of death and accountability.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The Day of Judgement is described in Surah al-Qari'ah as a day when men will be

options.B: made to drink from hot vessels
options.D: made to wear iron rods around their necks

explanation: Surah al-Qari'ah describes the terrifying chaos of the Day of Judgment, comparing scattered, disoriented mankind to moths dispersed in confusion, while mountains will be like carded, fluffed wool.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'Peace descending on the righteous throughout the night...' The verse above is one of the major themes in Surah
explanation: Surah al-Qadr describes Laylatul Qadr (the Night of Decree) as a night of peace and blessing that lasts until the break of dawn, marking it as the most sacred night in the Islamic calendar.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: A text of Hadith is rejected if it is

options.C: reported twice by the same reporter

explanation: One of the criteria scholars use to identify a fabricated or defective Hadith (matn criticism) is if its content contradicts established natural phenomena, sound reason, authentic Qur'anic teachings, or other well-established Hadith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: 'Indeed the people before you were destroyed by asking a lot of unnecessary questions…' The quotation above is in Hadith 9 of an-Nawawi which refers to the
explanation: Hadith 9 of an-Nawawi's collection warns Muslims against excessive questioning that leads to unnecessary complications, citing the People of the Book (Jews and Christians) as an example of communities destroyed by their persistence in raising unnecessary religious disputes.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Who among the six compilers of Hadith became blind before his death?
explanation: Imam at-Tirmidhi is reported to have wept so much out of fear of Allah in his later years that he became blind before his death, a testament to his deep piety alongside his scholarly contributions to Hadith literature.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: In Q.17:27, the extravagant are likened to associates of
explanation: Q. 17:27 states 'Verily, spendthrifts are brothers of the devils,' condemning wasteful and extravagant spending as behaviour associated with Satan, who himself was ungrateful to his Lord.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Islam decreed against begging through encouraging
explanation: The Prophet taught that it is better for a person to gather firewood and sell it than to beg from others, emphasizing the dignity and honour of earning one's living through honest labour rather than depending on charity through begging.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: What is expected of a believing Muslim when an important decision needs to be made?
explanation: Istikharah is the special prayer taught by the Prophet for a Muslim to perform when facing an important decision, seeking Allah's guidance to choose the path that is best for their religion and worldly affairs.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Fasting has been prohibited on a doubtful day which is the
explanation: Fasting is discouraged on the 'Day of Doubt' (Yawm ash-Shakk), which is the 30th of Sha'aban when the new moon has not been confirmed, to avoid unnecessarily beginning Ramadan a day early based on uncertain sighting.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The permission to marry up to four wives in Islam is aimed at

options.C: reducing the number of unmarried women

explanation: Polygyny in Islam is permitted, subject to the strict condition of equal and just treatment among wives, and among its social purposes is providing a lawful framework for marriage that helps prevent illicit relationships such as adultery.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: Lending out money with interest in Islam is
explanation: Riba (usury/interest) refers to any predetermined excess charged on a loan, which is strictly prohibited in Islam as it is seen as an exploitative practice that unjustly enriches the lender at the expense of the borrower.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
text: The outcome of the Prophet's visit to Cave Hira was

options.C: award of chieftaincy title by the Makkan aristocrats
options.D: his resolve to migrate to Madinah

explanation: It was during one of his periods of solitary contemplation in the Cave of Hira, at age forty, that the Prophet received the first revelation of the Qur'an through Angel Jibril, marking the beginning of his prophethood.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge
